meta.author: Llew (Lamarq7eYT)
meta.identity: Full-Stack Developer + ADS student, Brasil. Mistura código, design e animação. Especialidade em segurança web, 3D e experiências digitais imersivas.
meta.aesthetic: Cyber / hacker / investigativo. Escuro, agressivo, com personalidade. Não é portfólio — é uma experiência.

task: Build a cinematic, scroll-driven, frame-by-frame animated portfolio for Llew — a developer who blends code, security, 3D and motion design. This is not a traditional portfolio. It is a narrative experience styled like a creative studio AAA product.

stack.animation_primary: GSAP 3 (ScrollTrigger for scroll-driven timeline, timeline scrubber, frame control)
stack.styling: Tailwind CSS v3 + custom CSS variables
stack.fonts: JetBrains Mono (code/terminal) + Syne (display headings) — import from Google Fonts

typography.mono: JetBrains Mono, monospace — labels, code, terminal, debug panel

effects.glitch: CSS keyframe animation: translate + clip-path on ::before/::after pseudo-elements. Triggered at scroll milestones and on hover of key headings.
effects.scanlines: Repeating CSS linear-gradient overlay at 2px intervals, opacity 0.03, on body background.
effects.noise: SVG feTurbulence filter applied as a fixed ::before on body, opacity 0.04.
effects.crt_vignette: Radial gradient from transparent center to rgba(0,0,0,0.6) at edges, fixed position.

architecture.app: src/App.tsx — single scrollable page. No routing needed. All sections mounted in DOM simultaneously; GSAP ScrollTrigger manages visibility.

src/.App.tsx: Mounts all sections + GlobalEffects + DebugPanel + TimelineScrubber + PerformanceToggle

sections.name: Boot Sequence (Tela Inicial — Hook)
sections.description: Fullscreen dark terminal boot animation. Runs once on first load. Simulates a system initializing. After boot completes OR user presses any key, transitions to Hero with a hard-cut glitch flash. DO NOT autoplay if user has prefers-reduced-motion.
sections.implementation: Each line types in sequentially using setTimeout + requestAnimationFrame. Monospace font. Green (#00ff88) text. Blinking cursor after last line. Any keydown OR click triggers exit. Exit: GSAP timeline fades out with a horizontal glitch scanline wipe, then Hero fades in.

sections.name: Hero — Frame-by-Frame Animation Player
sections.description: The main cinematic entry. Contains a Three.js canvas as background (particle field, subtle fog). Foreground: the identity reveal + call to scroll. The 'frame-by-frame animation' concept is implemented as a GSAP ScrollTrigger timeline where scroll position maps to animation progress — giving the feeling that the user is scrubbing an animation.

content.headline: Llew
content.tagline: Criando experiências digitais onde código vira narrativa.

three_js_scene.description: Floating cyan/green particles on dark background. Particles drift slowly. On scroll, they accelerate and reorganize into the shape of a grid — visually representing 'system loading'. Use BufferGeometry + Points. Keep draw calls minimal.

sections.gsap_setup: Pin the hero section for 200vh of scroll. During this pinned period, GSAP animates: (1) the name 'Llew' glitch-reveals letter by letter, (2) particles converge, (3) subheadline slides in from left, (4) background shifts from full black to #090b0f. After pin ends, scroll continues to About.
sections.implementation_notes: Use GSAP ScrollTrigger.create() with pin: true, scrub: 1. All animations inside a master GSAP timeline controlled by the scrub. This is the core of the 'frame player' metaphor.

sections.name: About — Quem é Llew
sections.description: Split layout. Left: a terminal block showing identity as a JSON/code object (like his README). Right: skills displayed as a tech tree or animated tag cloud. Animates in as the section scrolls into view via GSAP from-opacity-0.

right_block_content.animation: Each skill tag fades and slides in with staggered delay (GSAP stagger: 0.05). On hover, tag glows with accent color border + subtle scale.

sections.name: Projects — Cenas Narrativas
sections.description: Each project is a 'scene' — not a card. The section uses horizontal snap-scrolling OR stacked full-viewport panels, each with its own atmosphere, color accent, and mini-narrative. The scroll continues the main GSAP timeline, making each project feel like a new animation chapter.

projects.name: AegisCore
projects.tagline: Plataforma híbrida TypeScript + Rust para defesa web aplicada.
projects.scene_description: Open with a 'threat detected' visual: red scanlines flash, terminal lines cascade showing attack vectors being blocked. Then calm — the system stabilized. This represents AegisCore neutralizing attacks.
projects.narrative_hook: O sistema estava sob ataque. Credential stuffing. Brute force. Session theft. Então AegisCore entrou em ação.
highlights: Arquitetura monorepo com apps/, packages/, native/
Módulos Rust via N-API: payload normalizer, request inspector, risk scoring
Rate limiting multidimensional, CSRF, RBAC/ABAC, session defense
Threat model completo documentado em docs/threat-model.md
projects.visual_element: Animated diagram of the request lifecycle flow (Boot → GSAP draws SVG path arrows between: Request → CORS → Session → CSRF → Auth → RBAC → Rate Limit → Handler → Audit Log)

projects.name: BreachRoom / HackerPage
projects.tagline: Ambiente imersivo sobre a história dos hackers. Sala investigativa digital.
projects.scene_description: The panel background becomes a dim blue-tinted room. Think crime board + terminal. Scattered files, glowing monitors implied through CSS shadows. A Three.js particle burst evokes network packet visualization.
projects.narrative_hook: E se você pudesse entrar dentro de um incidente hacker? Não ler sobre ele — vivê-lo.
highlights: Cena 3D imersiva com Three.js na web
Narrativa histórica sobre segurança da informação
Design investigativo: sala de análise, terminais, overlays
Resultado de 2 anos de estudo em 3D, TypeScript e design narrativo
projects.visual_element: Iframe embed of the live site OR a screenshot that fades in with a CRT monitor frame CSS effect

projects.name: AegisHub
projects.scene_description: Minimal, sharp. Dark amber accent. Terminal lines showing Rust compilation output. Represents raw performance and systems-level craft.
projects.narrative_hook: Onde JavaScript não é suficiente, Rust assume o controle.
highlights: Desenvolvido em Rust — linguagem de systems programming
Foco em performance, segurança de memória e precisão
projects.visual_element: Animated terminal showing 'cargo build --release' output with green OK lines

projects.name: Polybech
projects.scene_description: Clean, architectural. Grid lines. Blueprint aesthetic. Shows methodical engineering mindset.
projects.narrative_hook: Cada projeto começa com uma estrutura sólida.
highlights: Projeto TypeScript — estrutura e tipagem forte
Parte do ecossistema de projetos em construção
projects.visual_element: Animated TypeScript interface definition appearing token by token

sections.navigation: Between project scenes, a glitch transition wipes the screen. The accent color of each scene tints the top border and the particle color of the background Three.js scene.

sections.name: Área de Código Vivo
sections.description: A section that simulates live coding. Three tabbed snippets appear as if being typed in real time. Includes intentional 'errors' that get corrected mid-typing. Uses react-syntax-highlighter with a custom dark theme.

snippets.label: AegisCore — Risk Scoring
Three.js — Particle System
Rust — Payload Normalizer

sections.typing_behavior: Each snippet types at ~40ms per character. Tabs switch manually by clicking. Add a deliberate typo mid-way (e.g., 'cosnt' instead of 'const') that auto-corrects with a brief red flash — like a dev catching their own mistake.

sections.name: Contact — CTA Final
sections.description: The animation 'ends'. The scroll has reached the final frame. A cinematic moment: the section fades in slowly, the particles dissolve, a single line of text appears center screen. Then the CTA emerges.

cta_button.label: [ iniciar colaboração ]
cta_button.action: Opens mailto:link OR reveals contact info with a terminal-style decode animation
cta_button.animation: Button appears only AFTER all text lines have rendered. Uses GSAP timeline with staggered delays. On hover: green border glow + subtle horizontal glitch on text.

sections.footer_text: Llew · Full-Stack Dev · Brasil · Construindo o futuro, linha a linha.

interactive_features.name: Timeline Scrubber (After Effects vibe)
interactive_features.description: A fixed bottom bar — always visible — showing overall scroll progress as a timeline. Styled like a video editor timeline: thin track, playhead marker, timestamp. User can DRAG the playhead to jump to any section. Implemented by reading GSAP ScrollTrigger's totalProgress and allowing direct scrub via drag event.
interactive_features.ui: Fixed bottom: 0. Height: 40px. Background: rgba(9,11,15,0.95) with top border #1e2d3d. Left label: [FRAME 000/999]. Draggable playhead in green. Right: section name of current position.

interactive_features.name: Modo Debug (tecla D)
interactive_features.description: Hidden panel activated by pressing the 'D' key. Reveals technical metadata about each project as the user scrolls past it. Styled as a terminal overlay in the bottom-right corner. Dismissable with 'D' again or Escape.

aegiscore.architecture: Monorepo: apps/ packages/ native/ docs/ tests/
key_decisions: Rust via N-API para módulos críticos de performance
TypeScript como base da plataforma — melhor DX e manutenção
challenges: Bridge TypeScript ↔ Rust com N-API sem overhead excessivo
IDOR/BOLA prevention em endpoints com dados cruzados
Throttling adaptativo por score de risco em tempo real

key_decisions: Migrou de Spline para Three.js nativo — mais controle autoral
GitHub Pages para deploy estático sem backend
challenges: Construir atmosfera investigativa só com CSS + Three.js
Narrativa histórica acessível sem glorificar ataques

interactive_features.name: Modo Performance vs Qualidade
interactive_features.description: A small toggle button fixed top-right. Two modes: 'Cinemático' (full effects, all particles, all glitch layers) and 'Fluido' (reduced particles to 500, no glitch layers, no scanlines, simplified transitions). Detects prefers-reduced-motion on load and defaults to Fluido if true.
interactive_features.ui: Icon-only button. Lucide 'Zap' icon for Cinemático, 'Wind' for Fluido. Tooltip on hover.

eggs.effect: Terminal boots with 'ACESSO NÍVEL ROOT CONCEDIDO'. Reveals a hidden section with a 'projetos secretos / beta' message and confetti burst in green/cyan.

eggs.trigger: Click on 'Llew' text in hero 7 times
eggs.effect: Full screen glitch for 1 second, then screen resets with a subtle 'nice try' message in bottom corner that auto-disappears after 3s.
AegisCore status panel slides in from right: 'Sistema ativo. Nenhuma ameaça detectada. Sessão segura.' with green checkmarks.

interactive_features.name: Glitches Narrativos
interactive_features.description: At specific scroll milestones (entering Projects section, transitioning between projects), the site deliberately 'breaks': viewport flickers, a distortion layer flashes for 200-400ms, then reveals the next section cleanly. This is intentional — part of the narrative identity. Implemented as GSAP-triggered CSS class additions that activate a full-screen glitch overlay div.
interactive_features.glitch_overlay: A fixed full-viewport div, normally display:none, with z-index: 9999. When activated: applies CSS animation that cycles through: horizontal tear, color aberration (R/G/B channel offset via filter), random clip-path rectangles revealing different parts of the background, then fades out. Duration: 300-500ms total.

performance_requirements.three_js: Use a single shared WebGLRenderer across all scenes. Dispose geometries and materials when sections scroll out of view. Max 2000 particles in Cinemático mode, 500 in Fluido.
performance_requirements.gsap: Register only ScrollTrigger plugin. Use gsap.context() for cleanup in React useEffect.
performance_requirements.lazy_loading: Split each major section into its own React.lazy() chunk. Only Hero and BootSequence load eagerly.
performance_requirements.mobile: On screens < 768px: disable Three.js entirely (show static CSS gradient instead), reduce GSAP animations to opacity-only, hide TimelineScrubber, simplify glitch effects to CSS-only. Show a 'melhor em desktop' notice that can be dismissed.
performance_requirements.bundle: vite.config.ts must include: base: './', manual chunks splitting vendor (three, gsap, framer-motion) from app code. Target bundle: < 400kb gzipped for initial load.

localization.language: Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) for all user-facing text

deliverables: Complete React + Vite + TypeScript project in src/
All components listed in file_structure implemented
All 4 projects from data/projects.ts populated with real data from above
All 3 interactive features (DebugPanel, TimelineScrubber, PerformanceToggle) functional
At least 2 narrative glitch transitions implemented
vite.config.ts with base: './', chunk splitting, and build optimizations
README.md with: npm install, npm run dev, npm run build, explanation of keyboard shortcuts and easter eggs
index.html with proper og:title, og:description, og:image placeholder for Llew's portfolio
anti_patterns: NO generic portfolio layouts (grid of project cards with hover shadows)
NO purple gradients on white backgrounds
NO heavy animation without lazy loading or performance fallback
NO confusing UX — user must always know what to do next (scroll, press key, drag)
NO placeholder images — use CSS + Three.js + SVG for all visuals
NO lorem ipsum text — use real content from the projects data above
final_note: This portfolio should feel like it was made by someone who treats code as craft and design as identity. Every technical decision should be visible and intentional. The person reviewing this portfolio should feel: 'this dev builds things differently.' That is the only success metric.
